title: Scalp Cooling in Preventing Persistent Chemotherapy-Induced Alopecia: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
ground_truth_gap_statement: Existing studies of scalp cooling efficacy are limited by short-term duration and have not evaluated whether scalp cooling prevents persistent chemotherapy-induced alopecia (PCIA) months after chemotherapy ends.
ground_truth_hypothesis: Scalp cooling during chemotherapy reduces the incidence and severity of persistent chemotherapy-induced alopecia at 6 months after chemotherapy completion compared with usual care.
ground_truth_data_source: Open-label 2:1 randomized controlled trial in newly diagnosed stage I-III breast cancer patients receiving neoadjuvant or adjuvant chemotherapy (Dec 2020-Aug 2021), with hair thickness and density measured by Folliscope 5.0 and CIA-related distress measured with CADS
ground_truth_key_finding: At 6 months post-chemotherapy, PCIA occurred in 13.5% (12/89) of the scalp-cooling group versus 52.0% (26/50) of controls; scalp cooling increased hair thickness (mean difference 9.0 microns) and reduced CIA-related distress (CADS difference -3.2), with a small density difference (-3.3 hairs/cm).
ground_truth_future_work: Further research is necessary to observe longer-term benefits of scalp cooling beyond 6 months.

title: Breast Cancer Information Grand Round for Survivorship (BIG-S) prospective cohort.
ground_truth_gap_statement: Few existing cohorts comprehensively integrate patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and real-world healthcare utilization data, particularly in Asian populations, and a lack of an Asian breast cancer registry comparable to those in Western settings exists despite distinct demographic (~50% premenopausal in Asia vs 15-30% in the West) and tumor-microenvironment differences.
ground_truth_data_source: BIG-S prospective single-center cohort at Samsung Medical Center, recruiting newly diagnosed breast cancer patients from November 2018 (N=2,749); integrates EMR clinical data, bioelectrical impedance body composition, ICHOM-recommended PROs, and healthcare utilization records.
ground_truth_key_finding: Among 2,749 participants (mean age 49.7), mean QoL improved from 55.6 at diagnosis to 68.2 at 4 years, but fatigue and insomnia persisted, sexual functioning and future perspectives declined, and healthcare utilization shifted from plastic surgery/rehabilitation early on to gynecology, family medicine, and psychiatry after 2 years - a pattern suggesting delayed recognition of psychosocial and sexual health needs.
ground_truth_future_work: Use of BIG-S to guide targeted interventions and tailored survivorship-care strategies, especially for Asian breast cancer survivors; integration into early identification and management of persistent symptoms (fatigue, insomnia, sexual dysfunction).

title: Temporal patterns of chronic disease incidence after breast cancer: a nationwide population-based cohort study.
ground_truth_gap_statement: Prior studies have not provided detailed descriptions of the temporal pattern of chronic disease incidence after breast cancer due to limited sample sizes, short follow-up, and lack of information on pre-cancer risk factor levels, making it hard to separate the specific contribution of breast cancer and its treatments from baseline risk.
ground_truth_data_source: Nationwide retrospective cohort using Korean National Health Insurance Service (NHIS) claims and biennial health screening exam data (2002-2016): 84,969 incident breast cancer cases vs 1,057,674 age- and region-matched cancer-free controls; breast cancer treated as a time-varying exposure.
ground_truth_key_finding: Breast cancer was associated with increased risk of leukemia (HR 4.20), cardiomyopathy (HR 3.28), osteoporosis (HR 3.00), endometrial cancer (HR 2.70), and hypothyroidism, with distinct temporal patterns: some risks (cardiomyopathy, osteoporosis, hypothyroidism) peaked early and decayed while others (leukemia, endometrial cancer) increased over follow-up; risks for leukemia, cardiomyopathy, endometrial cancer, hypothyroidism, pulmonary fibrosis, and hyperlipidemia remained elevated beyond 5 years.
ground_truth_future_work: Define optimal strategies for prevention, early detection, and management of chronic conditions after breast cancer; incorporate surveillance and prevention into survivorship care; further study the impact of chemotherapy and hormone therapy on thyroid disorders.

title: Autologous vs. implant-based breast reconstruction and long-term mental disorder risk: a retrospective cohort study emulating a target trial.
ground_truth_gap_statement: Prior studies comparing autologous (AR) and implant-based (IBR) breast reconstruction focused largely on short-term aesthetic or psychological outcomes within 12 months, rarely examined clinically diagnosed mental disorders as long-term outcomes, lacked rigorous control for baseline mental health, and were limited to single-center or small cohorts, leaving the long-term psychological impact poorly understood.
ground_truth_hypothesis: AR would be associated with a higher long-term risk of clinically diagnosed mental disorders compared with IBR at 1, 5, and up to 9 years after reconstruction.
ground_truth_data_source: Korean National Health Insurance Service (K-NHIS) nationwide database 2015-2023, with 1:3 propensity-score matching (5113 AR vs 14 738 IBR among 24 930 eligible patients).
ground_truth_key_finding: AR was associated with a higher long-term risk of any clinically diagnosed mental disorder (aHR 1.13, 95% CI 1.07-1.19), with the strongest signal in anxiety (aHR 1.25) and a more pronounced effect in patients aged >=50 years (aHR 1.16); risk persisted through 5 years, while delayed AR in younger patients showed a reversed direction (aHR 0.79, interaction p=0.021).
ground_truth_future_work: Integration of mental health screening and age-sensitive, individualized counseling into preoperative decision-making; replication in other healthcare systems.

title: Less harmful alternative for smoking: addressing concerns on the observational study of electronic cigarettes and cardiovascular outcomes.
ground_truth_gap_statement: The widespread framing of e-cigarettes as a 'less harmful alternative' to combustible smoking is challenged by observational evidence of cardiovascular harm, but methodological concerns about that evidence (confounding, exposure definition, dual use) need to be addressed before causal claims can stand.
ground_truth_data_source: European Heart Journal editorial/comment on an observational study of electronic cigarettes and cardiovascular outcomes; no primary data.
ground_truth_key_finding: The editorial argues that observational evidence on e-cigarettes and cardiovascular outcomes should not be dismissed on methodological grounds alone, and that the 'less harmful alternative' framing requires scrutiny against accumulating real-world cardiovascular signals.
ground_truth_future_work: Improved observational designs (clearer exposure definitions, handling of dual use, causal-inference frameworks) and long-term cohorts to clarify cardiovascular risk of e-cigarettes.

title: Comprehensive Young Age Breast Cancer registry from clinical, genomics, and patient-reported outcomes measured with 15 years follow-up: the CHARM cohort profile.
ground_truth_gap_statement: Young breast cancer (YBC) patients face distinct clinical challenges and research gaps that are inadequately captured by existing breast cancer registries, which typically lack integrated clinical, multi-omics, fertility-related, and patient-reported outcome data with long-term follow-up.
ground_truth_data_source: Comprehensive Young Age Breast Cancer (CHARM) registry at Samsung Medical Center (since April 2013); patients <=40 at diagnosis or pregnant/postpartum within 1 year, with electronic medical record data, Medidata Rave EDC, central pathology review, annual bone density, multi-omics tumor/blood samples, reproductive hormone panels (AMH, estradiol, FSH, LH), and mobile PRO questionnaires
ground_truth_key_finding: The SMC YBC cohort enrolled 1,868 participants (mean age 35.57 years, 99.8% premenopausal), of whom 1,062 completed PRO questionnaires, establishing a comprehensive YBC platform to optimize care and knowledge for this underserved population.

title: Validation of the Korean Version of the Patient-Reported Outcomes Measurement Information System 29 Profile V2.1 among Cancer Patients
ground_truth_gap_statement: Generic profile HRQoL measures such as SF-36 and EQ-5D have documented limitations in cancer (missing cancer-relevant symptoms; not capturing all health-state aspects), while the newer PROMIS-29 V2.1 had only been validated in 'specific cancer patients' and had 'no psychometric evaluation' in a mixed cancer-survivor population, leaving Korean cancer patients without a validated generic profile measure.
ground_truth_hypothesis: The Korean version of PROMIS-29 V2.1 will demonstrate acceptable reliability, construct validity (via PCA and CFA), and convergent/discriminant validity with EORTC QLQ-C30 in Korean cancer patients.
ground_truth_data_source: Cross-sectional survey of 349 cancer outpatients at Samsung Medical Center Comprehensive Cancer Center (Sep-Oct 2018) completing K-PROMIS-29 V2.1 and EORTC QLQ-C30
ground_truth_key_finding: K-PROMIS-29 V2.1 showed satisfactory internal consistency (Cronbach's alpha 0.81-0.96), good model fit (CFI 0.91, SRMR 0.06, NNFI 0.90), and moderate-to-large convergent correlations with EORTC QLQ-C30 domains; however, depression and anxiety items loaded onto a single factor (r=0.80) and the sleep-disturbance domain split, suggesting the negative-affect overlap and the wording of 'refreshing' sleep need refinement.
ground_truth_future_work: Investigate hierarchical/multidimensional models of depression and anxiety to build more efficient computer adaptive tests, and conduct test-retest reliability studies in broader Korean cancer populations.

title: Surveillance of Symptom Burden Using the Patient-Reported Outcome Version of the Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events in Patients With Various Types of Cancers During Chemoradiation Therapy: Real-World Study.
ground_truth_gap_statement: Existing symptom-measure guidelines for cancer were designed for clinical trials and provide little guidance for real-world surveillance; prior PRO-CTCAE subsets have focused on common Western cancers (breast, lung, colorectal) and lack coverage of less common cancers such as lymphoma, stomach, and liver cancer, and differences in symptom profiles across cancer types have not been fully elucidated for real-world surveillance.
ground_truth_hypothesis: Symptom frequency and severity measured with cancer-specific PRO-CTCAE subsets differ by cancer type during chemotherapy or radiation therapy, and higher symptom burden is associated with poorer health-related quality of life.
ground_truth_data_source: Cross-sectional tablet-based PRO survey of 1,352 outpatients receiving chemotherapy and/or radiation therapy at the National Cancer Center (Goyang) and Samsung Medical Center (Seoul) between December 2017 and January 2018, using 10 cancer-specific PRO-CTCAE-Korean subsets developed via Delphi
ground_truth_key_finding: Gastrointestinal symptoms dominated across all cancer types; fatigue (76.5%), decreased appetite (65.4%), and numbness/tingling (57.5%) were the most frequent, with substantial site-specific patterns (e.g., decreased libido in >50% of colorectal, gynecologic, breast, and lung cancers; pain during sexual intercourse in 59.8% of gynecologic/lymphoma patients); worsening PRO-CTCAE scores were associated with clinically meaningful HRQoL declines (e.g., fatigue coefficient -8.15).
ground_truth_future_work: Holistic, multi-symptom monitoring and management strategies based on comprehensive PROs are needed in routine care, and the approach of linking routinely collected PROs to large population-based health care databases should be expanded.

title: Author Correction: Perceived stress and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease in apparently healthy men and women
ground_truth_gap_statement: The original article (10.1038/s41598-019-57036-z) inadvertently switched the legends of Figures 1, 2, and 3, creating a mismatch between figure content and described content that needed formal correction.
ground_truth_data_source: Author correction notice referring to the original Scientific Reports article (PMID 31913326) on the Kangbuk Samsung Cohort Study
ground_truth_key_finding: The figure legends for Figures 1-3 in the original NAFLD-stress paper were swapped; the corrected legends are provided so that Figure 1 shows the participant flowchart, Figure 2 shows the spline OR curve, and Figure 3 shows the subgroup OR comparison.

title: Quality of life, function, and symptom trajectory patterns following salivary gland cancer treatment: a 5-year prospective cohort study.
ground_truth_gap_statement: Longitudinal patterns of patient-reported quality of life (QoL) remain poorly understood in patients with salivary gland cancer (SGC), and baseline characteristics associated with adverse recovery patterns have not been well characterized.
ground_truth_data_source: Prospective single-center cohort at a tertiary cancer center from 2016, with 252 histologically confirmed SGC patients followed for up to 5 years using EORTC QLQ-C30 and H&N35 questionnaires; group-based trajectory analysis applied.
ground_truth_key_finding: Three distinct global QoL trajectories were identified - Stable Good (52.4%), Declining (16.2%), and Delayed Recovery (31.3%) - with female sex, older age, worse baseline pain, and recurrence predicting unstable trajectories, while treatment intensity was not a significant predictor.
ground_truth_future_work: Trajectory-based classifications may support early identification of vulnerable survivors and inform risk-stratified supportive care targeting pain, nutrition, and emotional function.
